task: 6-verdict UI-screenshot classification (app | obstructed | marketing | error | blank | non-app)
rationale: Versioned, model-agnostic distillation recipe for the on-device verdict classifier (#488 ruling, epic #490). The labels are the hosted-model verdicts already cached in the design-ref corpus (#489) — a base-model switch re-runs THIS recipe against the same labeled corpus rather than re-labelling. Keep it reproducible: bump `version`, append a dated `revisions` entry, re-run `design-refs.mjs export`, re-train.

student.note: ≲10 MB-quantized student per #488 F1/F2 — a classifier, not a VLM (the separate Tier-2 build).

distillation.teacher: hosted vision model verdicts cached in verdicts.json / per-item visionVerdict (#489)
distillation.note: Distil from the big-model labels; the recipe is teacher-agnostic — the teacher identity lives in each frame's visionVerdict.provider, not here (no-leakage boundary, #475).

graduationBenchmark.verdictAgreement: >=0.95 vs hosted model on the held-out split
graduationBenchmark.quarantineRecallFloor: >=0.98 vs hosted model on the held-out split
graduationBenchmark.metric: quarantine-recall = of held-out frames whose TRUE verdict is a quarantine-class (decideAdmission → quarantine: marketing/error/blank/non-app), the fraction the student ALSO routes to quarantine. Measured by `design-refs.mjs benchmark` (epic #490 slice B).
graduationBenchmark.note: Two independent floors. verdict-agreement (0.95) is the overall fidelity gate; quarantine-recall carries a HIGHER floor (0.98) because a quarantine miss admits junk into the corpus — a costlier, asymmetric error than a plain agreement miss. Meeting BOTH promotes the student from the API bridge (#485) to the bundled on-device default; the API stays as the premium upgrade (#488 F1/F4). The 0.98 floor is a defensible engineering default to be re-confirmed once a labeled holdout slice exists (today the corpus is all-ungated).

revisions.note: Initial recipe — codified from the #488 ruling (all five forks). Pairs with the design-refs.mjs `export` step (epic #490 slice A); benchmark floors finalized in slice B.

Slice B (#512): finalized the TBD quarantineRecallFloor at >=0.98 (asymmetric-error rationale above) and added the verdict benchmark harness (`design-refs.mjs benchmark` + design-refs/benchmark.mjs) that measures verdict-agreement + per-class quarantine-recall over the manifest held-out split. No re-train/re-label trigger — thresholds-only change.
